most remarkable interpreters generation, Claire Désert entered Conservatoire National Supérieur Musique Paris when just years old.
After studies with Ventsislav Yankoff Jean Hubeau, received premiers prix piano chamber music.
After admission postgraduate cycle, granted French government scholarship study year with Evgeniy Malinin Tchaikovsky Conservatory Moscow.
Upon return, completed second postgraduate year chamber music with Roland Pidoux.
addition numerous solo appearances international music festivals such Radio France Montpellier, Roque d'Anthéron, Piano Jacobins, Piano Lille Festival, ‘Folles Journées' Nantes, Bilbao, Lisbon Tokyo, Claire Désert also often appears with Orchestre Paris, Orchestre national d'Île-de-France, Orchestre philharmonique Radio France, Orchestre symphonique Québec, Orchestre philharmonique Strasbourg, Orchestre National Lille, Budapest Symphony Orchestra.
keen chamber musician, regularly partners such eminent artists Emmanuel Strosser, Anne Gastinel, Philippe Graffin Gérard Caussé, well Quatuor Sine Nomine Quintette Moraguès.
impressive discography includes Scriabin Dvorak concertos with Orchestre philharmonique Strasbourg (which received French ‘Victoire Musique' 1997), Brahms programme pianos with Emmanuel Strosser three programmes with cellist Anne Gastinel.
Recent releases include Schumann's Davidbündlertänze, Dvořák's Slavonic Dances piano duet with pianist Emmanuel Strosser, Abendmusik, solo album dedicated Clara Robert Schumann Brahms.
latest recording, Schumann's Fantasie Romances Waldszenen, awarded Choc-Classica, Joker Crescendo Magazine Supersonic Pizzicato.
